title: Async / Await
summary: async and await are a cleaner way to work with promises.
You mark a function async, then await a result inside it.
The code reads top to bottom like normal.

sections.text: Promises are powerful, but chains of then can get hard to read. Async and await are a newer style that lets you write code that waits, but reads as simply as if nothing was waiting at all.

sections.title: What async and await Do
sections.text: You mark a function with the word async. Inside it, you can use await before a slow task, which tells the code to pause right there until the result arrives, then continue. The function does not block the whole page, only its own flow waits.

sections.title: Why It Reads Better
sections.text: With await, asynchronous code looks like ordinary step by step code. Get the data, then use it on the next line, just as you would expect. It is still promises underneath, but the shape is far easier to follow than a chain of then calls.

media.alt: A chain of then calls beside the same logic written with await

sections.title: A To-Do List with One Pause
sections.text: Imagine a to-do list where one step says wait for the kettle to boil. With await, you simply write that line and carry on down the list once it is done. You do not need a tangle of notes about what to do later. The list just pauses at that one line, then continues.

media.alt: A to-do list pausing at one step, then continuing down the list

sections.title: Using async and await
items: Inside, write await before a slow task.
The code pauses there until the result arrives.
Use the result on the next line as normal.
Wrap it in try and catch to handle failures.

sections.text: await only works inside an async function, by design. It is a gentle rule that keeps your waiting code clearly marked, so anyone reading it knows where pauses can happen.

terms.definition: Marks a function that can use await.
Pauses the function until a result arrives.
What async and await use underneath.
Waiting at one line for a slow task.
How you handle failures with async code.

questions.text: What are async and await for?
options: A cleaner way to work with promises
questions.explanation: async and await are a cleaner, more readable way to work with promises.

options: Pauses the function until the result arrives
questions.explanation: await pauses the function's flow until the awaited result is ready.

questions.text: What runs underneath async and await?
questions.explanation: async and await are built on promises under the hood.
